rationale: The citing paper uses Danaher, Bonfrer, and Dhar (2008) in its theory framing to support a specific mechanism: competitive advertising clutter can reduce advertising effectiveness via interference. This mechanism is presented as a countervailing force to higher consumer attention at demand peaks, shaping the paper's conceptual setup and empirical question about how advertising elasticities vary over intrayear demand cycles. Although the cited study is not reused methodologically, it plays a meaningful explanatory role beyond a generic literature list mention.
relation_description: The citing paper draws on the cited paper's competitive advertising interference finding to motivate why advertising effectiveness may weaken when demand peaks coincide with heavier competitive advertising clutter. This prior evidence helps structure the citing paper's conceptual argument about opposing forces behind intrayear variation in advertising elasticity.
